description_long: This object is a vintage dual-lens magic lantern projector, featuring a rectangular wooden body with metal reinforcements and ornate brass fittings. Two prominent cylindrical brass lens housings protrude from one side, each equipped with a circular brass shutter. The top of the projector is capped with a black, fluted metal chimney, likely for heat dissipation, and brass handles are attached to the sides for carrying. The rear side displays a complex arrangement of pipes and valves, suggesting a mechanism for controlling airflow or fuel, while the opposite side has two circular access doors with brass rims. The base is broad and wooden, providing stability, and the overall construction is robust, with a mix of wood, brass, and iron elements.
description: A vintage dual-lens magic lantern projector with a wooden body, brass lens housings, and a fluted metal chimney on top. It features ornate fittings, side handles, and a complex rear mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden body, two protruding brass lens housings, and the fluted metal chimney on top.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the intricate network of pipes, valves, and brass handles on either side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying two circular access doors with brass rims and the lens housings extending from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the dual brass lens assemblies with circular shutters and the symmetrical arrangement of the projector's face.
attribute_lens_type: fixed; large cylindrical brass lenses with circular shutters, no visible adjustment rings
attribute_ventilation_pattern: A prominent fluted metal chimney on top serves as the main ventilation, with no additional vents or grills visible.
